snippet: Easy extensions are disappearing as office and multifamily owners face higher rates, tougher refinancing, and new workout strategies. ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå
subject: The Easy Extension Era Is Over
body:
----------
**In partnership with**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e082c8b1-32dc-4d06-9c7c-bc0ae792e7a7/grocapitusv2.png?t=1781188909)
Follow image link: (https://luma.com/z3lch2qo)
Caption: 


--------------------
**Good morning. **For years, struggling commercial real estate owners counted on lower rates to bail them out. Now, with a $1T debt wall looming, lenders are asking a tougher question: Is the asset worth saving?

‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî

üéôÔ∏è **This Week on No Cap: **Henry founder [Sammy Greenwall](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YUUcRav106c&amp%3Bfeature=youtu.be) explains why AI isn't replacing CRE professionals‚Äîit's eliminating the work they hate most.

Listen & subscribe: [Apple Podcasts](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/no-cap-by-cre-daily/id1756322834) | [Spotify](https://open.spotify.com/show/0elUh7oPKT8S9a1qngQN0z) | [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@NoCapCREDaily)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/adddd83f-e74c-4277-8a04-50a2153d72e0/Sammy_Greenwall_Thumbnail.png?t=1781105225)
Follow image link: (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YUUcRav106c&amp%3Bfeature=youtu.be)
Caption: 


--------------------
## **CRE Trivia **üßÝ

Which hedge fund manager personally earned roughly $4B in 2007 by shorting subprime mortgage-backed securities?


--------------------
**IN PARTNERSHIP WITH GROCAPITUS INVESTMENTS**

## What Should CRE Investors Watch Next?

View image: (https://sponsy.nyc3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/e0c03d02-14c6-4ee8-bdbb-3c63f800e98c/ws1wt-JUNE12CREDAILYAD.png)
Follow image link: (https://luma.com/z3lch2qo)
Caption: 

Commercial real estate is resetting. Join Neal Bawa for a live webinar on what investors should watch as the market shifts‚Äîand where opportunities may emerge next.

Save Your Spot (https://luma.com/z3lch2qo)

*This is a paid advertisement. Please see the full disclosure at the bottom of the newsletter.


----------## CRE Workouts Enter a More Forced Phase

The [CRE distress cycle](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/cre-loan-workouts-shift-focus-as-debt-maturities-loom/) is entering a tougher chapter as lenders shift from extending loans to determining which properties are worth saving.

The debt wall is here: Roughly $1T in CRE debt matures this year, forcing borrowers into a refinancing market with higher rates and stricter lender expectations. Loans originated at 3%‚Äì4% are now resetting closer to 7% or more, creating valuation gaps that many owners can't easily bridge, according to the Urban Land Institute.

Lenders want solutions: Workout experts say the days of quietly hoping for lower rates are fading. Borrowers who communicate early, understand their markets, and present credible business plans stand a better chance of avoiding foreclosure, bankruptcy, or receivership. As Fox Rothschild's Brett Axelrod put it, lenders expect owners to be "part of the solution rather than the problem."

Capital stacks get crowded: Unlike previous downturns, today's distressed deals often involve a web of CMBS debt, mezzanine financing, EB-5 capital, preferred equity, and private credit. More stakeholders mean more competing interests, making restructurings slower and more complicated.

Receiverships gain traction: Panelists highlighted the growing use of consented receiverships, in which lenders appoint third-party operators to stabilize troubled assets while funding completion or repositioning efforts. Rather than signaling failure, receiverships can buy time and preserve value as longer-term restructuring plans take shape.

Easy money wanted: While traditional banks remain cautious, private lenders and distressed debt investors are chasing opportunities created by refinancing pressure and falling valuations. But many "distressed" buyers are really looking for stabilized assets at discounted prices‚Äînot the complexities of true workouts.

Multifamily joins the watch list: Office remains the largest source of distress, but multifamily is becoming a growing concern, particularly in high-growth Sun Belt markets like Austin. Slowing rent growth, rising operating costs, and large development pipelines are squeezing owners whose projects were underwritten with more optimistic assumptions.

**‚û• THE TAKEAWAY**

Prove it or lose it: The CRE story is shifting from "extend and pretend" to "prove and perform." Owners who bring realistic valuations, transparent communication, and fresh capital to the table may preserve value, while those waiting for market conditions to bail them out could find lenders making the next move.

----------
**A MESSAGE FROM CREXI**

## Crexi AI. See it work on a real deal.

Follow image link: (https://crexi.com/ai?utm_campaign=ai&utm_source=credaily&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_content=intel_secondary_jun12)
Caption: 

Crexi AI is the intelligence layer built into your workflow ‚Äî and the June 23 session is built for everyone evaluating, funding, or valuing a deal. Watch it move through the full evaluation workflow live: sourcing properties, reading the market, running valuation ‚Äî every figure traceable to its source in the Crexi Network. Register at crexi.com/ai.

Register Free (https://crexi.com/ai?utm_campaign=ai&utm_source=credaily&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_content=intel_secondary_jun12)

*This is a paid advertisement. Please see the full disclosure at the bottom of the newsletter.


--------------------
* [Tax savings](https://costsegregationguys.com/credaily): Maximize depreciation with the nation‚Äôs leading cost segregation firm, 25% less than competitors. Cost Segregation Guys completed 10,000 studies and $1B in depreciation last year. Get a free analysis today! _(sponsored)_

* [Calpers shift](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/calpers-launches-600b-total-portfolio-investing-overhaul/): CalPERS launches a $600B total portfolio approach removing asset-class silos to optimize returns, putting pressure on CIO Stephen Gilmore to meet long-term targets.

* [Fraud fallout](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/california-investor-charged-in-100m-real-estate-loan-fraud/): US prosecutors charged a California investor with bank fraud over nearly $100M in real estate-backed loans tied to falsified collateral, heightening concerns about credit quality at regional banks.

* [Free to start](https://nordicrealestateservices.com/?utm_source=credaily&utm_medium=review&utm_campaign=solsten_review_2026&utm_content=primary_cta): Get professional-grade underwriting, forecasting, and risk analysis without the cost or complexity of legacy platforms. _(sponsored)_

* [Rates reset](https://knowledge-leader.colliers.com/steig_seaward/quick-hits-rising-yields-extend-cre-pricing-reset/): Higher Treasury yields are sustaining a higher-rate CRE environment, pushing borrowing costs and cap rates upward and extending the ongoing valuation repricing cycle.



* [Housing push](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/new-york-city-tops-us-for-new-multifamily-construction/): NYC apartment construction is surging due to strong demand and policy changes despite sub-2% vacancy rates and high development costs.¬Ý

* [Student merger](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/scion-group-acquires-student-quarters-1-5b-platform/): Scion Group is acquiring Student Quarters‚Äô $1.5B operating platform, adding 29 assets and nearly 13,000 beds across major U.S. university markets.¬Ý

* [Holy housing](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/high-rents-push-new-yorkers-into-convents-boarding-houses/): Soaring NYC rents are pushing young professionals into convent residences that offer affordable rooms, meals, and community but come with strict rules like curfews, chores, and limited privacy.¬Ý

* [Grad relief](https://www.globest.com/2026/06/11/top-markets-for-new-grads-show-how-far-landlords-will-go-to-fill-units/): Landlords in major U.S. metros are offering widespread concessions to attract new grads, keeping rents relatively affordable despite strong demand.

* [Data ban](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/seattle-moves-to-ban-new-data-center-construction-citywide/): Seattle is moving to ban large data center development, becoming the biggest U.S. city to restrict AI-driven facilities amid concerns over rising electricity costs, water use, and grid strain.

* [Factory gap](https://www.globest.com/2026/06/11/companies-talk-up-new-plants-while-cutting-back-on-factory-spending/): Manufacturers announced major U.S. plant plans, but factory construction spending is falling as automation and outsourcing keep output steady without new building growth.

* [Industrial premium](https://www.connectcre.com/stories/marcus-millichap-closes-deal-for-bronx-industrial-with-redevelopment-potential/): A Bronx industrial site sold for $10.3M, highlighting strong investor demand for scarce NYC logistics land as industrial uses command premiums.

* [UTC deal](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/urw-acquires-full-control-of-san-diego-s-westfield-utc-mall/): URW will acquire the remaining 50% of Westfield UTC for $705M, gaining full control of the San Diego mall as it continues a luxury retail repositioning..¬Ý

* [PPI surge](https://www.connectcre.com/stories/producer-prices-rise-sharply-in-may-as-goods-inflation-accelerates/): U.S. producer prices rose 1.1% in May, lifting annual wholesale inflation to 6.5% as goods costs drove most of the increase.¬Ý

* [Fun surge](https://www.bisnow.com/national/news/retail/entertainment-tenants-become-retails-new-anchor-134963): Family entertainment centers are rapidly expanding across the U.S., filling vacant big-box retail space and reshaping mall traffic.

* [Cava expansion](https://www.bisnow.com/national/news/retail/cava-plans-to-open-75-new-stores-expand-headcount-134948): Cava will open 75+ new restaurants and hire 2,500 workers by year-end as it accelerates U.S. growth toward a 1,000-location target.

* [Midtown buy](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/werner-lands-midtown-office-at-330m-discount-to-2019-price/): David Werner bought One Dag in Midtown Manhattan for $270M, a steep discount from its prior $600M sale.

* [Spirit auction](https://www.bisnow.com/south-florida/news/office/spirit-airlines-hq-to-hit-the-auction-block-next-month-134955): Spirit Airlines‚Äô South Florida HQ will be auctioned in July after bankruptcy, with buyers able to bid on the campus or portions of it.¬Ý

* [Silver foreclosure](https://www.bisnow.com/washington-dc/news/office/silver-spring-office-building-headed-to-foreclosure-sale-134954): A 96K SF Silver Spring office building is headed to a foreclosure auction on June 17 after a loan default tied to a 2019 refinance.

* [Old sale](https://www.credaily.com/briefs/dc-s-waldorf-astoria-sells-for-80m-spurs-buyer-interest/): The federal government sold Washington‚Äôs Old Post Office building (Waldorf Astoria) to BDT & MSD Partners for $80M, with the buyer already exploring a resale at a higher valuation.¬Ý

* [SEC settlement](https://www.bisnow.com/dallas-ft-worth/news/hotel/phoenix-american-hospitality-and-ceo-settle-sec-case-over-86m-investment-scheme-134952): Phoenix American Hospitality and its CEO settled an SEC case over an alleged $86M scheme, paying penalties and accepting restrictions without admitting wrongdoing.

* [Luxury stays](https://www.costar.com/article/859313874/guests-remember-how-we-make-them-feel-fairmont-and-raffles-ceo-says): Accor‚Äôs CEO says luxury hotels are shifting toward personalization and emotional guest recognition, focusing on experiences and destinations to drive repeat stays despite rising rates.


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/adbd859a-9c0d-4766-a536-f67f53d445de/image.jpg?t=1781189381)
Follow image link: (https://www.linkedin.com/posts/carlwhitaker15_do-yall-know-the-scene-in-indiana-jones-share-7469748692997099521-Fm5W/)
Caption:  

Office vacancies have [climbed above](https://www.a16z.news/p/charts-of-the-week-rto-stalled) 14%‚Äîtheir highest level since the Global Financial Crisis‚Äîas persistent hybrid work and sluggish job growth deepen the divide between thriving newer buildings and struggling older offices.

## **CRE Trivia (Answer)**üßÝ

John Paulson of Paulson & Co. His fund generated approximately $15B on the trade, one of the largest single-year profits in hedge fund history.
